signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.19; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Implication: risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme long positioning. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7172; 25d RR at −0.08: mild put skew. Implication: late-trend caution signal as market hedging rather than chasing. Watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3527; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. Implication: BoE rate hike expectations support GBP.

signals.title: USD/CAD — CB + Technical
signals.text: The BoC at 2.25% vs Fed at 3.75% — 150bp CAD carry disadvantage. USD/CAD at 1.3647, testing the 1.3650 level. The bear steepener yield curve regime (+58.2bp 2s10s spread) suggests a risk-off environment, which typically favors the USD. Positioning data shows a net short 63,570 contracts (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), aligned with the carry read. Watch 1.3700 as the next resistance level.
